notable_pick_trades: Acquired 3rd-round pick (68) from Jets via Haason Reddick trade. Acquired 5th-round pick (153) from Falcons. Traded away own 3rd-round pick to Dolphins for Jaelan Phillips (who left in FA). Traded 6th-round pick to Ravens for Jaire Alexander. Awarded 4 compensatory picks (Rd 3-98, Rd 4-137, Rd 5-178, Rd 6-215).
roster_context: The Eagles won Super Bowl LIX after the 2024 season but regressed to 11-6 in 2025 with a Wild Card loss to the 49ers. The offense stalled badly under coordinator Kevin Patullo (since replaced by Sean Mannion), leading the league in three-and-outs. The defense remained elite under Vic Fangio but lost three key starters in free agency (EDGE Jaelan Phillips, S Reed Blankenship, LB Nakobe Dean). A.J. Brown trade discussions are tabled for now but remain fluid — a post-June 1 trade is widely expected. Dallas Goedert re-signed on a 1-year deal. OL starters Lane Johnson and Landon Dickerson both confirmed returns despite retirement speculation. Howie Roseman is balancing a championship-caliber roster core with significant cap management challenges.

needs.explanation: Lost Jaelan Phillips ($120M deal with Panthers), Joshua Uche (Dolphins), and Azeez Ojulari (Falcons) in free agency — three edge rushers gone. Signed Arnold Ebiketie on a 1-year, $7.3M prove-it deal, but ESPN noted the Eagles 'will continue to look for edge rushing help.' Current group of Nolan Smith Jr., Jalyx Hunt, and Ebiketie are solid rotational pieces but lack an elite pass-rushing presence. Brandon Graham may return for a 17th season but is a depth piece at best. This is the single biggest hole on the roster.
needs.current_starter: Nolan Smith Jr., Arnold Ebiketie (FA signing)
needs.depth: Jalyx Hunt (2nd-year), Brandon Graham (potential return, age 38). Very thin after losing 3 edge rushers.

needs.explanation: Reed Blankenship signed a 3-year, $24.75M deal with the Texans. He was the full-time starting safety for three seasons and a team captain. The Eagles invested a 2025 2nd-round pick in Andrew Mukuba, but he is unproven. Michael Carter II had his contract renegotiated and could see looks at safety. There is no clear starting-caliber safety on the roster to pair with C.J. Gardner-Johnson's replacement (CJGJ was traded in 2025). This is a critical need.
needs.depth: Michael Carter II (versatile DB, could play safety), Brandon Johnson (practice squad callup). Very thin.

needs.explanation: A.J. Brown remains on the roster for now, but trade discussions with the Rams and Patriots were described as 'serious' before being tabled. A post-June 1 trade is widely expected due to cap implications ($43.5M dead cap before June 1 vs. $16.4M after). Even if Brown stays, the WR room behind him and DeVonta Smith is barren — Jahan Dotson signed with the Falcons. The Eagles met with WR prospect Omar Cooper Jr. at the Combine, signaling they may draft a replacement proactively. The offense led the league in three-and-outs in 2025 and desperately needs more explosive passing-game weapons.
needs.current_starter: A.J. Brown (trade situation fluid), DeVonta Smith
needs.depth: Quez Watkins (PS return), John Metchie III (acquired from Texans). Extremely thin if Brown is traded.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 if Brown is traded, Round 3-4 regardless for depth

needs.explanation: Nakobe Dean signed a 3-year, $36M deal with the Raiders. Zack Baun is an elite LB1 but the LB2 spot is now a question mark. 2025 1st-round pick Jihaad Campbell has a serious shoulder injury that will keep him out for most of the offseason, per DC Vic Fangio. The Eagles called the Steelers about a trade for Patrick Queen. This is a real need given Dean's departure and Campbell's injury concerns.
needs.current_starter: Zack Baun (LB1), Jihaad Campbell (LB2, injured — shoulder)
needs.depth: Jeremiah Trotter Jr. Campbell's shoulder injury (had prior surgery) is concerning. Eagles actively seeking additions.

needs.explanation: Lane Johnson (turns 36 in May) confirmed his return but suffered a Lisfranc injury that cost him the final 8 games of 2025 — the Eagles went 3-5 without him. He is elite when healthy but his age and injury history make succession planning critical. Jordan Mailata is locked in at LT. The team lost backup OL Brett Toth (49ers) and Matt Pryor (Cardinals). Mock drafts consistently project the Eagles targeting an OT in Round 1 to develop behind Johnson.
needs.current_starter: Jordan Mailata (LT), Lane Johnson (RT, age 36, returning from Lisfranc)
needs.depth: Fred Johnson (re-signed as backup), Hollin Pierce (practice squad). Very thin after Toth and Pryor departures.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 — potential succession plan for Lane Johnson

needs.explanation: Landon Dickerson confirmed his return after retirement speculation, but had a brutal 2025 dealing with knee surgery, back, and ankle injuries (PFF grade 67.2, 30th among guards). Cam Jurgens struggled with back surgery recovery and had the highest blown run block rate among starting centers. Tyler Steen is adequate but unspectacular at RG. New OL coach Chris Kuper replaces the legendary Jeff Stoutland. The starting 5 returns intact, but depth was gutted and the health/performance of Dickerson and Jurgens are genuine concerns. Drew Kendall (2025 draft pick) provides center depth.
needs.current_starter: Landon Dickerson (LG, injury concerns), Cam Jurgens (C), Tyler Steen (RG)
needs.depth: Drew Kendall (backup C), Jake Majors (PS). Lost Matt Pryor and Brett Toth.

needs.explanation: Dallas Goedert re-signed on a 1-year, $7M deal (announced March 15). He had a career-high 11 TDs in 2025 but is 31 years old and on a short-term contract — the Eagles need to find his long-term replacement. Re-signed Grant Calcaterra and added blocking TE Johnny Mundt. The tight end group lacked quality blocking in 2025, contributing to a significant dip in run game production. Multiple mock drafts project the Eagles targeting a TE like Kenyon Sadiq or Justin Joly.
needs.depth: Grant Calcaterra (re-signed), Johnny Mundt (signed, blocker), E.J. Jenkins. Goedert is a short-term solution.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-3 — depending on A.J. Brown situation, could go TE at 23

needs.explanation: The Eagles invested heavily here in free agency, signing Tariq Woolen (1-year, $15M) and Jonathan Jones (1-year deal) to join All-Pros Quinyon Mitchell and Cooper DeJean. This was a need before free agency with Adoree' Jackson and Kelee Ringo as the CB3 options, but Woolen and Jones address it for 2026. Both are on 1-year deals though, so a long-term CB3 could be a draft target. The secondary is a strength.
needs.depth: Tariq Woolen (signed), Jonathan Jones (signed), Kelee Ringo. Well-stocked for 2026 but Woolen/Jones are on 1-year deals.

free_agency_impact: Lost EDGE Jaelan Phillips (Carolina Panthers, 4yr/$120M) — EDGE becomes tier-1 critical need
Lost S Reed Blankenship (Houston Texans, 3yr/$24.75M) — Safety becomes tier-1 critical need
Lost LB Nakobe Dean (Las Vegas Raiders, 3yr/$36M) — LB2 need increases
Lost WR Jahan Dotson (Atlanta Falcons, 2yr/$15M) — WR depth gutted
Lost EDGE Joshua Uche (Miami Dolphins, 1yr) — further thins edge group
Lost EDGE Azeez Ojulari (Atlanta Falcons, 1yr) — further thins edge group
Lost OL Brett Toth (San Francisco 49ers) — OL depth hit
Lost OL Matt Pryor (Arizona Cardinals) — OL depth hit
Lost QB Sam Howell (Dallas Cowboys) — backup QB departure, Tanner McKee remains
Re-signed TE Dallas Goedert (1yr/$7M) — TE1 retained but still need long-term solution
Re-signed TE Grant Calcaterra (1yr) — TE depth retained
Re-signed P Braden Mann (4yr/$14M) — punter locked up long-term
Signed CB Tariq Woolen (1yr/$15M from Seahawks) — CB no longer top need
Signed CB Jonathan Jones (1yr from Commanders) — CB depth added
Signed EDGE Arnold Ebiketie (1yr/$7.3M from Falcons) — partial EDGE replacement, still need more
Signed TE Johnny Mundt (1yr from Jaguars) — blocking TE addition
Extended DT Jordan Davis (3yr/$78M through 2029) — DT locked up, not a need
Renegotiated DB Michael Carter II contract — cap savings, versatile DB retained
Renegotiated OL Landon Dickerson contract (2yr/$36M revised) — LG retained despite retirement talk
A.J. Brown trade discussions tabled after 'serious conversations' with Rams and Patriots — could heat up around June 1
scheme_fit_notes: Vic Fangio's defense values versatile defensive backs who can play multiple roles (safety/slot), making a safety who can also cover the slot extremely valuable. His 4-3 under scheme needs edge rushers who can set the edge in the run game AND rush the passer — pure speed rushers are less ideal. On offense, new OC Sean Mannion is installing a Shanahan-style outside zone scheme, which elevates the importance of athletic offensive tackles who can move in space and tight ends who can both block and create mismatches. Lane Johnson's successor needs to be a zone-blocking fit. The run-heavy identity means the Eagles value blocking at every position, including TE and WR.
